LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-23-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT EDGEWOOD PARTNERS INSURANCE CENTER'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. Off Calendar Jul-30-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Discovery
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT EDGEWOOD PARTNERS INSURANCE CENTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Edgewood Partners Insurance Center's (dba Epic Insurance Brokers and Consultants) motion to strike plaintiff Doe Garcia's first amended complaint (FAC) is denied as moot. Defendant's demurrer was sustained with leave to amend.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Sustained

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EDGEWOOD PARTNERS INSURANCE CENTER D/B/A EPIC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Patrick Frye's unopposed application to appear as counsel pro hac vice for Edgewood Partners Insurance Center an Oak Hill Capital Partners is granted.

Order signed

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS EDGEWOOD PARTNERS INSURANCE CENTER D/B/A EPIC, AND OAK HILL CAPITAL PARTNERS' MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.
